title: Google Brings Agentic AI and Vibe-Coded Widgets to Android
summary: Google announced major Gemini AI upgrades at its Android Show event, giving the assistant the ability to complete multi-step tasks across apps, browse the web autonomously, and let users build custom widgets using natural language.
content: Google announced a suite of new Gemini Intelligence features at its Android Show: I/O Edition event, enabling the AI assistant to handle complex multi-step tasks like copying a grocery list from one app and adding items to a shopping cart in another — with the phone screen acting as live context. Gemini will also fill out forms on behalf of users, browse the web autonomously to complete tasks like booking appointments, and arrive on Android's Gboard keyboard via a dictation feature called Rambler that transcribes speech and strips filler words. In a notable creative addition, users will be able to 'vibe-code' their own Android widgets by describing what they want in plain language — for example, requesting a meal-planning widget that suggests weekly recipes. These features will initially roll out to Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel devices this summer, then expand to other Android hardware, watches, cars, and laptops later in 2026.

title: EU Clinches Deal to Roll Back AI Restrictions, Delaying High-Risk Rules to 2027
summary: EU legislators struck a landmark deal to postpone enforcement of the AI Act's high-risk AI provisions until December 2027, marking the first significant rollback of digital rules in the bloc's history.
content: The EU reached a provisional agreement to delay enforcement of its AI Act's high-risk provisions by more than a year, pushing the compliance deadline from August 2026 to December 2027 for most sectors, and to August 2028 for AI embedded in physical products. The deal largely exempts industrial AI applications — a major concession to Germany, whose Chancellor Friedrich Merz pushed to protect companies like Siemens and Bosch from a double regulatory burden. Negotiations ran through the night in marathon sessions, concluding around 4:30 a.m. on May 7, with the Cypriot Council presidency and European Parliament confirming agreement. The deal also includes a new ban on AI systems that generate sexualized deepfakes and child sexual abuse material, effective December 2026, responding to global outrage over Grok-generated content. Commission President Ursula von der Leyen called the agreement 'a simple, innovation-friendly environment' for AI in Europe, as the bloc faces pressure from the US and its own industry over competitive disadvantage.

title: Isomorphic Labs Raises $2.1 Billion to Scale AI Drug Design Engine
summary: DeepMind spin-off Isomorphic Labs secured a $2.1 billion Series B round led by Thrive Capital to expand its AI-powered drug discovery platform, with backing from Alphabet, the UK Sovereign AI Fund, and other major investors.
content: Isomorphic Labs, an AI-first drug design company founded by Sir Demis Hassabis in 2021, announced a $2.1 billion Series B funding round to scale its unified AI drug design engine (IsoDDE) and accelerate its therapeutic pipeline toward the clinic. The round was led by Thrive Capital with participation from Alphabet, GV, MGX, Temasek, CapitalG, and the UK Sovereign AI Fund. IsoDDE works across multiple therapeutic areas and drug modalities, providing predictive fidelity designed to identify viable candidates with unprecedented speed — recently validated through published benchmarks bridging structure prediction and real-world drug discovery. The company maintains strategic partnerships with Novartis, Lilly, and Johnson & Johnson, and plans to use the new capital to hire AI, engineering, drug design, and clinical talent globally. Hassabis said the funding marks the point where Isomorphic moves from proving its approach to 'scaling our technology to its full potential' in pursuit of the mission to 'solve all disease.'

title: OpenAI Launches $14 Billion Deployment Company to Embed AI Engineers in Enterprises
summary: OpenAI launched the OpenAI Deployment Company, a new enterprise arm valued at $14 billion and backed by $4 billion in investment from 19 leading firms, designed to embed specialized AI engineers directly into client organizations.
content: OpenAI announced the launch of the OpenAI Deployment Company, a majority-owned subsidiary that will place specialized Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) inside client organizations to build and deploy AI systems for critical business workflows. The company launched with over $4 billion in investment from a partnership that includes TPG, Bain Capital, Goldman Sachs, SoftBank Corp., McKinsey & Company, and 15 other leading firms, yielding a reported $14 billion valuation. As part of the launch, OpenAI agreed to acquire Tomoro, an applied AI consulting firm with approximately 150 engineers who have worked on production AI systems for Tesco, Virgin Atlantic, and Supercell. FDEs will work directly with business leaders to redesign workflows around AI — moving organizations from use-case selection through to live production deployment — connecting OpenAI models to customers' data, tools, and processes. OpenAI framed the initiative around the belief that over one million business deployments have made clear that 'real impact comes from helping people and organizations use those systems safely, effectively, and at scale.'

title: Google Identifies First AI-Developed Zero-Day Exploit and Thwarts Planned Mass Cyberattack
summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group discovered what it believes is the first zero-day exploit built with AI assistance and prevented the criminal group's planned mass exploitation event before it could be deployed.
content: Google's Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has identified the first zero-day exploit it believes was developed using AI — a Python script that bypasses two-factor authentication in a popular open-source system administration tool — and disrupted the criminal group's planned mass exploitation event before deployment. The exploit bore clear LLM fingerprints: hallucinated CVSS scores, educational docstrings, and textbook-style formatting, and it targeted a semantic logic flaw that traditional scanners miss but that frontier models can surface through contextual reasoning. The GTIG report also documents Android malware called PROMPTSPY that uses Google's Gemini API to autonomously navigate victim devices, capture biometric data, and block uninstallation attempts using real-time AI-generated touch coordinates. State-sponsored actors from China, North Korea, and Russia are described as using AI for vulnerability research at industrial scale — with Chinese groups priming models using a database of over 85,000 real-world vulnerability cases to guide targeted code analysis. Google's defensive Big Sleep AI agent, built by DeepMind and Project Zero, discovered the flaw first — demonstrating that AI is now actively deployed on both sides of an accelerating cybersecurity arms race.

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Realtime-2 and Two New Voice API Models
summary: OpenAI released three new real-time voice models for developers: GPT-Realtime-2 with GPT-5-class reasoning, a live 70-language translation model, and a streaming transcription model built for low latency.
content: OpenAI released three new voice models in its API: GPT-Realtime-2, which brings GPT-5-class reasoning directly into live audio interactions; GPT-Realtime-Translate, a live translation model supporting over 70 input languages and 13 output languages; and GPT-Realtime-Whisper, a streaming speech-to-text model designed for real-time, low-latency transcription. Unlike previous approaches that relied on separate transcription and synthesis steps, GPT-Realtime-2 processes audio end-to-end with reasoning inside the audio loop, enabling voice experiences that can listen, reason, and take action as a conversation unfolds rather than simply responding to queries. GPT-Realtime-2 is priced at $32 per million audio input tokens and $64 per million output tokens, while the translation and transcription models are priced per minute at $0.034 and $0.017 respectively. Early customers already deploying the new models include Zillow, Intercom, Priceline, Vimeo, and Deutsche Telekom, spanning real estate, customer support, travel, and media. The launch represents OpenAI's push to move realtime voice from 'simple call-and-response toward voice interfaces that can actually do work.'
